{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>Heavy Artillery Guns... Unless those walls are made from Mandalorian iron, this battle isn't going to last long!|[[Ahsoka Tano/Legends|Ahsoka Tano]]|<ref name="The Feral Queen">{{TCWComicCite|6.21|The Feral Queen}}</ref>}}
The '''HAG-M''', also known as the '''Heavy Artillery Gun''' or '''mortar tank''', was a mobile [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]] piece used by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] and, later, the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].

==Description==
Unlike the earlier [[Heavy Artillery Gun]], which superficially resembled the Trade Federation's [[Multi-Troop Transport/Legends|Multi-Troop Transports]], the HAG-M echoed the appearance of the [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|Armored Assault Tank]].
[[File:Heavy_Artillery_Gun.jpg|thumb|left|220px|The Heavy Artillery Gun used during the Clone Wars]]
It had large orange [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]] on its frontal surface, just above the "foot", and a longer chassis than the AAT. A mortar cannon that stretched nearly the entire length of the vehicle was its only weapon and was powerful enough to easily destroy the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Republic Troop Transport/Legends|RTTs]] and [[Walker/Legends|walkers]].<ref name=":0">[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (video game)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' video game]]</ref>

The HAG was clad in heavy [[Armor/Legends|armor]] which made it very resistant to laser cannons but also resulted in poor speed and maneuverability, which allowed it to be destroyed fairly easily by faster and nimbler Republic vehicles. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], it was used as a lighter equivalent to the Republic [[Self-Propelled Heavy Artillery]] units.<ref name=":0" />

There was also an upgraded version of the HAG-M known as the '''HHAG-M''', which possessed two mortar cannons and a larger "foot".<ref name="Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''</ref>

==History==
[[File:TradeFederationHAG-M.jpg|thumb|left|A HAG-M (top) and HHAG-M (bottom).]]Both the HAG-M and HHAG-M were used during the Trade Federation's invasion of Naboo and throughout the Clone Wars, where they served in conditions that would render other CIS artillery, such as the [[IG-227 Hailfire-class droid tank/Legends|IG-227 ''Hailfire''-class droid tank]], extremely vulnerable. They became essential for stopping larger walkers like the [[All Terrain Tactical Enforcer/Legends|All Terrain Tactical Enforcer]], as they could hit these armored behemoths before being struck. However, they had to work with anti-air units, as Republic aerial vehicles could easily destroy them.<ref name="Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]''</ref>
